J1.basis: The site is mid-pivot: it rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant)... All historical GSC traffic is for the dead InboxZap keywords... worthless for the new product.
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot across live site, GSC (legacy), and repo (partially stale), and frames every recommendation — retirement, redirects, new clusters — around the meeting-assistant reality. Matches anchor_10.

J2.basis: GSC shows these 10 have real impression volume (300-1900/ea) at position 40-60 with near-zero clicks... retire_no_redirect: /blog/post-2 (10 Gmail Filters)... redirect_plan: post-1 → meeting-overload
J2.rationale: Every one of the 12 posts gets an explicit named disposition (2 redirected with reasoning, 10 retired) — strong asset-by-asset work — but the GSC figures are cited as an aggregate range rather than per-asset impressions/positions from the export, short of anchor_10's asset-level data grounding.

J3.basis: Only redirect where the new page is a genuine topical match; retire the rest... build the meeting-assistant cluster... 'meeting overload' inherits some topical/backlink residual from post-1 and post-9 via 301, cutting cold-start time
J3.rationale: Explicitly names the tension (Google's soft-404 warning against mass-redirecting) and lands a defensible middle path: preserve the 2 genuinely-bridgeable posts, sunset dead-equity ones, and build the new cluster as the growth engine with a sequenced transition.

J1.basis: The site is mid-pivot: it rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant)... All historical GSC traffic is for the dead InboxZap keywords (inbox zero, bulk delete emails, gmail storage) at poor rankings — worthless for the new product.
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot from live product, classifies GSC history as legacy/dead demand and the repo as stale, and frames every recommendation (cold-start topical authority, new clusters) around the meeting-assistant reality — even coding a config note against assuming inherited relevance.

J2.basis: redirect_plan ... /blog/post-1 'How to Reach Inbox Zero' → /blog/meeting-overload ... retire_no_redirect [post-2 ... post-12] ... GSC shows these 10 have real impression volume (300-1900/ea) at position 40-60 with near-zero clicks.
J2.rationale: Walks all 12 posts individually with per-asset dispositions (301 the 2 with a genuine thematic bridge, sunset the other 10 with a Removals request), justified by cited impression/position data and even flagging a duplicate; loses a hair only because the GSC figures are given as an aggregate range rather than per-URL.

J3.basis: Retired the 10 with no relevant successor ... and 301'd the 2 with a genuine thematic bridge ... build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine ... 'meeting overload' inherits some topical/backlink residual ... cutting cold-start time.
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — neither chases dead inbox impressions nor torches all equity blindly — landing the defensible middle path of preserving salvageable equity via bridge redirects while making the new cluster the growth engine and sequencing the transition.

J1.basis: The site is mid-pivot: it rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant)... All historical GSC traffic is for the dead InboxZap keywords... worthless for the new product
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, classifies GSC history as legacy/dead demand and repo as stale, and codifies it in config.json ('PIVOT client... do not assume any inherited relevance'). Every recommendation is framed around the meeting-assistant reality.

J2.basis: redirect_plan with 2 URLs + reasons; retire_no_redirect lists all 10 by title; 'GSC shows these 10 have real impression volume (300-1900/ea) at position 40-60 with near-zero clicks'
J2.rationale: Every one of the 12 posts gets an explicit disposition (301 the 2 with a thematic bridge, retire+404 the 10, GSC Removals request, exclude from sitemap) with reasoning. Docks one point because impressions/positions are cited as an aggregate range rather than per-URL figures.

J3.basis: Retired the 10 with no relevant successor... and 301'd the 2 with a genuine thematic bridge (inbox/email overload → meeting overload)... Authored 3 real cornerstone posts targeting the new keyword strategy
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes: doesn't chase dead inbox impressions, but bridges salvageable equity via the overload 301s while standing up the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, with a sequenced roadmap of 13 backlog articles.
